text:
User: for truncation we should prefer to remove the in-between assistante messages, never user messages -- by in-between I mean assistant messages that are followed by assistant messages. [...] ## What changed

**New strategy:** when a session exceeds the capture budget, instead of tail-slicing the head (which dropped the user's initial requirements), it now **drops only "in-between" assistant turns** — assistant turns immediately followed by another assistant turn (tool-call narration / intermediate steps). **User turns are never dropped**, and the final assistant turn of each run is kept. Dropping is oldest-first, and it only happens when actually over budget. [...] **New strategy:** when a session exceeds the capture budget, instead of tail-slicing the head (which dropped the user's initial requirements), it now **drops only "in-between" assistant turns** — assistant turns immediately followed by another assistant turn (tool-call narration / intermediate steps). **User turns are never dropped**, and the final assistant turn of each run is kept. Dropping is oldest-first, and it only happens when actually over budget. [...] - `tail_capped(s, max_bytes)` — char-boundary-safe tail-keep, replacing the raw byte slice that could panic mid-codepoint on emoji and abort capture. [...] 
The `tail_capped` backstop now fires only in the pathological case where surviving (mostly user) content alone exceeds budget — documented as intentional in a comment. [...] - `reduce_turns_to_fit(turns, max_chars, numbered)` — the turn-level reduction. The `numbered` flag adds the `NNNN| ` per-line prefix overhead to the cost model so the budget reflects the line-numbered EXTRACT input (your advisor's catch — otherwise the backstop would re-trim the head). [...] Assistant: Now the numbered/EXTRACT build — reduce at turn level so `numbered_transcript`, `transcript_lines`, and `transcript_roles` all derive from the same set:
 [...] **`src/capture.rs`** — both truncation sites:
- Triage input: `reduce_turns_to_fit(&turns, 200_000, false)` + `tail_capped` backstop.
- EXTRACT input: `reduce_turns_to_fit(&turns, 250_000, true)`, then `numbered_transcript` / `transcript_lines` / `transcript_roles` all built from that **same reduced set** — preserving the absolute-line-number invariant that `evidence_is_valid` / `author_for_ranges` / `cite` depend on. Confirmed those are the only three consumers.
